json_data.title: Preparation for the Major
json_data.description: Complete the following courses with grades of C– or better:

options.title: Musicianship
courses: ETHNMUS M6A - Introduction to Global Musicianship

options.title: Musical Cultures of the World
courses: ETHNMUS 20A - Musical Cultures of World: Europe and Americas
ETHNMUS 20B - Musical Cultures of World: Africa and Near East
ETHNMUS 20C - Musical Cultures of World: Asia

options.title: World Music Performance Organizations, Private Instruction in Music, and World Music Specializations
Advanced Private Instruction in Music
courses: ETHNMUS 162 - Advanced Private Instruction in Music

options.title: Capstone
options.description: Students must fulfill the capstone final project requirement (4 units) through a public recital (performance).  Students must  enroll in Ethnomusicology 199 (2 units) and pass a recital permission  jury. Instrumental  and vocal performers must present a portion of their recital performance, and composers must present excerpts from their recital scores in front of two faculty members. Students also enroll in Ethnomusicology 186 (2 units) during the term in which they perform their recital or their composition(s) are performed.

options.title: Performance/Composition Capstone
courses: ETHNMUS 199 - Directed Research or Senior Project in Ethnomusicology

options.title: Public Ethnomusicology Capstone
courses: ETHNMUS 195B - Community or Corporate Internships in Public Ethnomusicology

options.title: Scholarly Research Capstone
courses: ETHNMUS 199 - Directed Research or Senior Project in Ethnomusicology

options.title: Independent Capstone
courses: ETHNMUS 199 - Directed Research or Senior Project in Ethnomusicology
